summary: All 13 acceptance criteria verified against source code with high confidence. Implementation is thorough with proper test coverage.

results.title: SessionStart hook parses stdin JSON and extracts session_id

evidence.detail: consumeStdin() reads raw stdin, then JSON.parse(rawStdin) parses it into hookInput typed as HookInput which has session_id?: string
HookInput interface declares session_id?: string; env_file?: string
Conditional check: if (hookInput.session_id) gates all session setup
5 test cases cover: valid session_id+env_file, session_id without env_file, empty stdin, invalid JSON, missing session_id

results.title: SessionStart bridges CLAUDE_SESSION_ID to env via CLAUDE_ENV_FILE

evidence.detail: if (hookInput.env_file) { bridgeSessionId(hookInput.session_id, hookInput.env_file); }
bridgeSessionId() appends 'export CLAUDE_SESSION_ID=<id>' to the env_file, with guards for empty params and try/catch for non-blocking failure
4 test cases: writes to env file, empty envFile, empty sessionId, invalid path -- all pass without throwing
createSessionDir() creates .specweave/state/sessions/{sessionId}/ with recursive mkdir, writes lock.json with pid, started_at, last_heartbeat, increments[]
getSessionsDir and getSessionDir confirm correct path construction
Tests verify directory creation, lock.json content (pid, increments, timestamps), and idempotent re-creation

results.title: Dead sessions are garbage collected (dead PID + age > 5min)

evidence.detail: gcDeadSessions(projectRoot, maxAgeSeconds=300) iterates session dirs, reads lock.json, checks PID liveness via process.kill(pid,0), removes when !pidAlive && ageSeconds > maxAgeSeconds. Also removes orphaned dirs without lock.json and corrupted lock.json dirs older than threshold
gcDeadSessions(projectRoot) called during session start; logs count if > 0
5 test cases: dead PID removal, live PID preservation, orphaned dir removal, empty sessions dir, young dead PID preservation (age < threshold)

results.title: Auto-mode.json written to per-session dir when CLAUDE_SESSION_ID available

evidence.detail: printStartMessage reads process.env.CLAUDE_SESSION_ID; when set, computes autoModeFile = stateDir/sessions/{sessionId}/auto-mode.json and creates session dir if needed; when unset, falls back to stateDir/auto-mode.json
Session marker JSON includes sessionId: sessionId || undefined, written to the per-session autoModeFile path
handleCancelAuto reads CLAUDE_SESSION_ID from env; when set, checks per-session auto-mode.json path first, falls back to global
After removing flag file (line 110), cleans legacy state files (lines 113-131), then removes entire per-session directory via fs.rmSync(sessionDir, {recursive:true, force:true}) (lines 133-143)
handleAutoStatus reads CLAUDE_SESSION_ID from env; when set, checks per-session path .specweave/state/sessions/{sessionId}/auto-mode.json first, falls back to global .specweave/state/auto-mode.json
Reads and parses the resolved autoFlagPath (per-session or global) as AutoModeFlag

results.title: Stop hook reads session_id from stdin and uses per-session auto-mode.json

evidence.detail: Reads stdin into STDIN_DATA via cat, extracts CLAUDE_SESSION_ID=$(echo $STDIN_DATA | jq -r '.session_id // ""')
SESSION defaults to global auto-mode.json; if CLAUDE_SESSION_ID is set and per-session auto-mode.json exists, SESSION is overridden to sessions/{id}/auto-mode.json path

results.title: LockManager no longer auto-skips in VSCode environments

evidence.detail: shouldSkipLocks() only checks SPECWEAVE_DISABLE_LOCKS, SPECWEAVE_FORCE_LOCKS, and CI env vars (CI, GITHUB_ACTIONS, GITLAB_CI). No VSCODE_PID, TERM_PROGRAM, or VSCODE_IPC_HOOK detection. Comment on line 17-19 explicitly documents the intentional removal
Three explicit tests verify locks are NOT skipped when VSCODE_PID, TERM_PROGRAM=vscode, or VSCODE_IPC_HOOK are set
import { FileLock } from '../../utils/file-lock.js'
writeState() creates FileLock(this.stateFile), calls lock.acquire(5) with 5s stale threshold, writes via atomic temp+rename pattern in try/finally with lock.release()
Test 'should protect writeState with FileLock' verifies addActive works through the FileLock wrapper
Standalone module exporting FileLock class with acquire(staleLockSeconds) and release() methods. Uses atomic mkdir for cross-platform locking, PID-based stale detection, and recursive cleanup
Re-exported from canonical location for backward compatibility: export { FileLock } from '../utils/file-lock.js'
Imported from the reusable location: import { FileLock } from '../../utils/file-lock.js'

results.title: All per-session lookups fall back to global path when session_id unavailable

evidence.detail: getAutoModePath(sessionId, projectRoot): when sessionId is falsy or session dir doesn't exist, returns global .specweave/state/auto-mode.json
When CLAUDE_SESSION_ID is undefined, autoModeFile = stateDir/auto-mode.json (global)
When sessionId is falsy, autoFlagPath = global path
When sessionId is falsy, autoFlagPath = global path
SESSION defaults to global; only overridden when CLAUDE_SESSION_ID is non-empty AND per-session file exists
Two tests verify fallback: 'nonexistent' sessionId and undefined sessionId both return global path

results.title: AutoModeFlag type has optional sessionId field

evidence.detail: AutoModeFlag interface includes 'sessionId?: string' on line 94 with comment 'Claude Code session that owns this auto session'
Session marker written with sessionId: sessionId || undefined, confirming runtime usage of the optional field

unitTests: tests/unit/hooks/session-start-session-state.test.ts (8 tests: session setup, GC logging, error handling)
tests/unit/core/session/session-state-manager.test.ts (15 tests: path helpers, createSessionDir, getAutoModePath, bridgeSessionId, gcDeadSessions, touchSession, claimIncrement)
tests/unit/utils/lock-manager.test.ts (VSCode env tests: 3 tests confirming locks NOT skipped)
integrationTests: tests/integration/hooks/stop-auto-v5.test.ts (stop hook behavior including session marker handling)
